config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Society matters a lot, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because once you make “society” the priority, it is too easy for governments or majorities to excuse abuse, censorship, or discrimination. In my view, a healthy society is built by protecting each person’s dignity and freedom first, while still limiting those rights only when there is a clear and necessary public interest. I believe the balance should favor the individual unless there is a strong and specific reason not to.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya di Amerika Serikat, sejarah menunjukkan bahwa ketika “demi masyarakat” dipakai sebagai alasan utama, itu sering menjadi pintu bagi pemerintah atau mayoritas untuk membatasi kebebasan berbicara, beragama, dan hak minoritas. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak mudah dikorbankan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru lahir dari perlindungan kuat terhadap individu, bukan dengan menjadikan individu sekadar alat bagi tujuan kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with putting society first as a general rule, because in Indonesia I have seen how “for the common good” can be used to pressure dissent, silence minorities, or excuse unfair treatment. Society is not an abstract thing separate from people; if individual dignity and rights are weakened, the society itself becomes brittle and easy to abuse. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case, because public safety, disaster response, and basic order sometimes require limits. But the default should still be to protect the person first, and only override that with a clear, specific, and truly necessary public interest.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena menurut saya hak individu tidak boleh otomatis dikalahkan oleh “kepentingan masyarakat.” Dari pengalaman dan nilai yang kuat di Amerika Serikat, saya melihat bahwa alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, penindasan minoritas, atau perluasan kuasa pemerintah. Memang ada situasi nyata seperti keselamatan publik, bencana, atau ketertiban umum di mana pembatasan hak bisa dibenarkan, tetapi itu harus sempit, spesifik, dan benar-benar perlu. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menuntut individu tunduk dulu, melainkan yang melindungi martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang sebagai dasar bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to "the public interest" can be used to pressure people into silence, especially minorities or those who disagree with the majority. A society is only healthy when it protects individual dignity first, and then limits rights only in narrow cases like safety, disaster response, or urgent public order. If we reverse that order, it becomes too easy for power to hide behind the word "society."

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika Serikat, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika “demi masyarakat” dijadikan alasan utama, itu sering berujung pada sensor, penindasan minoritas, atau perluasan kekuasaan negara yang berlebihan. Saya setuju bahwa ada keadaan terbatas seperti keselamatan publik, bencana, atau ketertiban umum yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan hak, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, spesifik, dan benar-benar perlu. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru dibangun dari perlindungan tegas terhadap martabat dan kebebasan tiap individu, bukan dengan menempatkan individu di bawah kolektif secara default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
